binding: Founder decisions 2026-07-20: (1) oracle mode = DUAL-HASHED; (2) Wave 1 target branch = helm/execution-schedule-wave-1; (3) TEL-001 demoted INTEGRATED -> REMOTE_DELIVERED pending target-branch reachability; (4) five-state delivery vocabulary adopted. Revision 2 was incomplete (REQ-GOV-003, REQ-ES-002 absent) and used a vocabulary that could not express 'delivered but not to the declared target'.
purpose: Persist open claims as governed state rather than conversational memory.

rules.evidence_required: Every status above OPEN must cite a full 40-char SHA, a test result, or an artifact path.
rules.id_uniqueness: Every claim_id resolves to exactly one title. Enforced by tests/unit/test_open_claims_register.py.

id_namespaces.PKG-W1-*: Wave 1 schedule packages, ID mirrors HELM-W1-*
id_namespaces.REQ-*: requirement IDs as named in goal_state.json

delivery_state_definitions.AUDITED: artifact bytes independently reviewed; no commit implied
delivery_state_definitions.REMOTE_DELIVERED: exact commit reachable from SOME remote branch
delivery_state_definitions.TARGET_INTEGRATED: exact commit reachable from the DECLARED target branch
delivery_state_definitions.CI_VERIFIED: CI evaluated that exact commit and passed
delivery_state_definitions.ROADMAP_COMPLETE: roadmap package exit criteria met and separately evidenced
delivery_state_definitions.no_state_implies_the_next: Six distinct evidence states. Each requires its own evidence. AUDITED does not imply committed; REMOTE_DELIVERED does not imply target; CI_VERIFIED does not imply complete.
delivery_state_definitions.history: Rev2 vocabulary had a bare INTEGRATED that conflated REMOTE_DELIVERED with TARGET_INTEGRATED. Rev3 split them. Rev4 adds AUDITED and ROADMAP_COMPLETE.

revision_1_defect_record.what_happened: Revision 1 stated the rule 'claim IDs must resolve to exactly one claim' and violated it three lines later. TEL-004 was used for (a) authoritative domain aggregation in its own body, (b) 'Founder Live hardening' in GOV-001's consequence text, and (c) implicitly the voice slice via a false collision warning. Six items were absent entirely: HELM-W1-001, -003, -004, -005, REQ-GOV-002, N3_VERIFY.
revision_1_defect_record.why_it_matters: The register's stated purpose was to stop transcript memory being lossy. It was lossier than the transcript, because it was written from memory of the conversation rather than from the roadmap and goal-state files.
revision_1_defect_record.found_by: independent review (Grok 4.5), confirmed by machine check against files
revision_1_defect_record.correction: This revision. Completeness is now machine-checked against docs/helm/roadmap/HELM_EXECUTION_SCHEDULE_WAVE_1.md, coordination/goal/goal_state.json, and coordination/goal/build_to_goal_status.json.
revision_1_defect_record.false_statement_retracted: Revision 1's ID_COLLISION_WARNING asserted 'both W1-002d meanings renamed to TEL-004 and TEL-005'. That was false — TEL-005 is W1-002e, and the voice slice received no ID at all.

w1_002d_disposition.problem: W1-002d was bound twice in the 2026-07-20 transcript: first to the voice/founder-visible slice (P1), later to authoritative domain aggregation (P0).

resolution_option_A.label: HELM-W1-003
resolution_option_A.meaning: Founder Live Governance and Truth Hardening
resolution_option_A.now: PKG-W1-003 — a distinct package, never a W1-002 slice

w1_002d_disposition.do_not: Do not cite W1-002d. Do not collapse PKG-W1-003 into TEL-004.

claims.title: Stop verifier and burndown laundering mtime into evidence age

evidence.root_cause: runtime_truth_contract.is_fresh(kind, produced_at_epoch) takes a float and raises TypeError on None. Absent provenance is unrepresentable, so callers substitute mtime.
evidence.consequence: The 90% / 100% burndown figures were computed against ages a git operation can reset.

claims.blocking: Contract needs an explicit UNKNOWN path first. Expect the headline percentage to FALL — doctrine working, not regression.
claims.next_action: Extend runtime_truth_contract with UNKNOWN, then repair the three consumers

claims.title: Authoritative domain aggregation — a domain cannot be healthier than its required readings

evidence.detail: domain_verdict returns OBSERVED if ANY reading advances. GitCollector is in domain 'engineering' and sets observed_at=_now() unconditionally. Supervisor 6d stale, workers stale, models UNKNOWN, telemetry 17d stale — engineering still renders OBSERVED on nothing but a readable repo.
evidence.severity: High — confirmed fake-green path in shipping code
evidence.test_coverage: NONE. test_collectors.py tests only homogeneous domains.

claims.blocking: Blocks runtime-truth promotion. Does NOT block TEL-001 (no filesystem-time reintroduced).
claims.next_action: Define required vs optional readings per domain. Tests: git OBSERVED + all authoritative stale -> not OBSERVED; optional live + required UNKNOWN -> UNKNOWN; all required live -> OBSERVED

claims.title: Reject producer timestamps beyond a permitted clock-skew window

evidence.detail: age_seconds = max(0.0, read_at - observed_at). A future-dated timestamp clamps age to 0.0 -> OBSERVED -> LIVE indefinitely for arbitrarily old content. One hour of clock skew suffices.
evidence.severity: High — confirmed fake-green path in shipping code
evidence.test_coverage: NONE. No test passes a valid future timestamp.

claims.blocking: Blocks runtime-truth promotion.
claims.next_action: produced_at > now + permitted_skew -> UNKNOWN, reason=future_producer_timestamp. Tests: +30s accepted; +1h UNKNOWN; +30d UNKNOWN; future ts cannot remain LIVE as clock advances

claims.title: knowledge_engine mtime -> Freshness verdict (same class, outside P0 scope)

evidence.detail: age = (_now() - mtime).total_seconds() from abspath.stat().st_mtime, fed straight into Freshness(status, ...). Unmitigated instance of the audited rule.
evidence.note: Not in the P0 five. The '22 remaining P0 sites' figure is a SCOPE metric, not a completeness metric.

claims.title: Shared Git index permits cross-agent commit-scope contamination

evidence.incident: 2026-07-20: `git add` of 2 files produced a 3-file commit (27243127, abandoned unpushed). Corrected to 6065882786ee...
evidence.cause: INDETERMINATE — stale staged content and concurrent staging by another agent fit the evidence equally well. A .git/index.lock from a concurrent process was observed minutes later. Do NOT record this as proven stale-index.
evidence.invariant: A shared Git index is mutable global state. No agent may assume exclusive ownership of staged content.
evidence.mitigation_built: scripts/scoped_commit_gate.sh — detects contamination AFTER staging. Defense in depth, not the primary control.

claims.blocking: Blocks safe start of TEL-002.
claims.next_action: Adopt per-agent git worktree, GIT_INDEX_FILE, or a serialized repo lease. Worktrees eliminate the class rather than detecting it.

claims.blocking: Must not be folded into TEL-001 — that would retroactively widen an audited boundary.

evidence.found_by: cold review 2026-07-20; confirmed by dual-hash record (committed hash is null)

claims.next_action: None — resolved by tracking HELM_EXECUTION_SCHEDULE_WAVE_1.md in HEAD commit 8d86f9b5e212ec3b56f166e0ae7cb7bf679ab5cb.
claims.detail:
RESOLVED 2026-07-20. The roadmap oracle is tracked and committed on the active branch, establishing full durable version control.
  worktree sha256   39edf04a7180d83ca3cd52329accdb307788f89f736cc29dc7eacfe0c524eec2 (295 lines)
  committed sha256  39edf04a7180d83ca3cd52329accdb307788f89f736cc29dc7eacfe0c524eec2
Reconstructability and durable provenance are now satisfied.
claims.verification_method: git show <commit>:<path> piped to sha256sum, plus line-level diff.
resolution_options: A. Commit the 295-line worktree copy on a branch reachable from HEAD. [RESOLVED]

target_binding.unresolved_condition: f959efb2 is NOT an ancestor of github/helm/execution-schedule-wave-1 (target still at 797f9d71). Do NOT mark CLOSED.

metrics_reference.note: promotion_eligibility reports UNKNOWN while any P0 mtime site remains. It counts the five P0 files only — TEL-006 proves the true repo-wide inventory is larger.

w1_002_decomposition.note: HELM-W1-002 has no single PKG claim because the founder split it into slices on 2026-07-20. The decomposition is recorded here so a cold reader can resolve it without the transcript.

slices.TEL-004: audit finding D1, domain aggregation (was mislabeled W1-002d)
slices.TEL-008: W1-002d first binding, voice / founder-visible surfaces

w1_002_decomposition.critical_path_rule: TEL-001 + TEL-002 + TEL-003 must all pass before REQ-ES-004 may be marked satisfied. TEL-008 is serialized behind that package.

branch_bindings.rule: TARGET_INTEGRATED requires reachability from wave_1_target. Reachability from delivery_branch alone is REMOTE_DELIVERED.

oracle_identity.why: Committed-only ignores live operational state; working-tree-only is not reproducible from git history. Both are recorded so a later rewrite invalidates the evidence automatically. Discovered when a worktree verified against COMMITTED goal oracles while the register was built from WORKING-TREE ones — same green, different data.

oracles.note: self; hash is of revision 2 as read. Revision 3 supersedes it.
UNTRACKED — no committed baseline exists. The completeness authority for this register has no version history. Flagged as a standing weakness.

LIVE FILE — a running process rewrites it. working_tree_sha256 may already be stale. Any verification citing this record must re-hash and compare. Under SNAPSHOT_COPY the live source is NOT the promotion oracle; the pinned snapshot is.

LIVE FILE. Committed version says GOAL_REACHED/100%; working tree says RUNNING/90% with N3_VERIFY on HOLD. The divergence is the finding. Under SNAPSHOT_COPY the live source is NOT the promotion oracle; the pinned snapshot is.

PROMOTION_VERIFICATION.requires: same hashes observed independently by builder and reviewer

verification_classes.rule: Never mix the classes. A freshness window supports monitoring; it cannot establish that two reviewers evaluated the same oracle content.

live_file_tension.supersedes: QUIESCED_SNAPSHOT (rev 5) — snapshot-copy preserves live operation while still giving promotion verification a stable, reviewable evidence set

superseded_options.A_quiesce: stop the writer for the verification window; strongest pin, operationally intrusive
superseded_options.B_freshness_window: treat live oracles as streaming; assert age < SLA rather than byte identity
superseded_options.C_snapshot_copy: copy live oracles to an immutable snapshot at run start; pin the snapshot

live_file_tension.rationale: Freshness remains a RUNTIME-HEALTH control, never a content-identity control. Quiescence is valid but operationally heavier.
live_file_tension.atomicity_protocol: hash source -> copy -> hash snapshot -> hash source again. Accept ONLY when source_sha256_at_capture == snapshot_sha256. The second source hash MAY differ; that drift is recorded, not hidden.
live_file_tension.window_sizing_finding: A short stability sample produces FALSE CONFIDENCE. build_to_goal_status.json showed three distinct hashes within ~15 minutes on 2026-07-20 (09a3d1e1 -> c21b15ef -> 0727e8d5), so its writer cycles in minutes. A 2-second two-sample check would have passed ~99% of the time while proving nothing. The gate therefore REFUSES to certify unless the window exceeds a DECLARED writer period, or the writer is confirmed stopped.
live_file_tension.builder_boundary: Snapshot capture IS builder-executable (unlike quiescence, which would require stopping a process on the founder's system).
live_file_tension.required_gate: CAPTURE COHERENCE: source_sha256_at_capture == snapshot_sha256. NOT long-term source stability. A changed post-copy source hash does NOT invalidate the snapshot.
live_file_tension.stability_gate_relationship: scripts/oracle_stability_gate.sh remains a valid capability but is NOT the promotion gate under SNAPSHOT_COPY. It verifies QUIESCENCE, not snapshot coherence. It becomes mandatory only if the founder changes policy to QUIESCED_SNAPSHOT.

revision_2_defect_record.what_happened: Revision 2 omitted REQ-GOV-003 and REQ-ES-002 — both blocking, agent-owned, on the goal_state critical path. Its completeness test contained zero references to the requirements array, checking only current_critical_path_blocker (one scalar) and founder_only_actions_pending (one list). 25 requirements and the whole critical_path array were unchecked while the suite was described as file-backed completeness.
revision_2_defect_record.found_by: cold review, 2026-07-20; confirmed by machine check
revision_2_defect_record.also_found: TEL-001 recorded as INTEGRATED when reachable only from a non-target remote branch.

revision_3_note: Rev 3 recorded REQ-GOV-003/REQ-ES-002 in wording that could be misread as 'requirement missing'. Rev 4 corrects: the requirements are present and STALE; the register's claims were what was absent.
revision_4_note: Rev 5 closes the live-oracle question by founder decision (quiesced snapshot), adds the stability gate, and raises GOV-004 for roadmap oracle durability — which the dual-hash record exposed by recording committed_blob_sha256 as null.

Regenerate evidentiary sections from new runtime evidence

sequence.precondition: ONLY after the new evidence exists

critical_path.ordering_rationale: authority first for repository history, then execution, then evidence, then updated conclusions
critical_path.note: architectural uncertainty is no longer the principal risk. Loss of uncommitted work is.

critical_path_note: Quiescing the writer is NOT on the critical path unless the founder changes oracle policy from SNAPSHOT_COPY to QUIESCED_SNAPSHOT.

confirmation the reviewer read it without builder mediation

recorded_head_binding.invariant: register.recorded_head == git rev-parse HEAD == remote branch head

GOV-005_uncontrolled_repository_advancem.defect: HEAD changed without exclusive commit authority, without session attribution, without register reconciliation, without review-state invalidation.
GOV-005_uncontrolled_repository_advancem.consequence: Any review pinned before that window went stale on landing.
GOV-005_uncontrolled_repository_advancem.control_limit: DETECTION + ATTRIBUTION, NOT MUTUAL EXCLUSION. Git offers no server-side lease here; any actor with shell access can still commit. The lease fails closed and leaves an audit trail — it does not prevent.
GOV-005_uncontrolled_repository_advancem.was: recorded as GOV-005 in rev 9 (same meaning)

GOV-006_register_stale_against_repositor.defect: Rev 8 asserted '.gitignore uncommitted / nothing pushed' while the repo had already advanced and been pushed.
GOV-006_register_stale_against_repositor.was: recorded as part of GOV-005 in rev 9

GOV-007_commit_attribution_insufficient.defect: git author/committer == 'Michael Hoch' on ALL commits including the builder's, because git config is the founder's. Metadata cannot distinguish Michael, Antigravity, another agent, an automation, or a concurrent session.

GOV-008_scoped_commit_control_bypass.proof_of_failure_mode: coordination/governance/evidence/gitignore.pre_worktree.bak — a builder rollback copy, not a governance artifact — was admitted by directory-wide staging.

GOV-009_unverified_index_lock_deletion.explicit: Absence of the lock NOW is NOT evidence its removal was safe. Not reconstructable.

GOV-010_reviewer_environment_assumed_not.defect: Cold review has now failed TWICE on reviewer-environment reachability. Attempt 1: worktree placed outside the mount. Attempt 2: reviewer has NO mount at all. Both times the builder designed a protocol around an unverified assumption about the reviewer's filesystem. The Option B analysis asserted 'any path under the mount is reachable by BOTH sides' — the builder verified only its own side.
GOV-010_reviewer_environment_assumed_not.builder_error_class: Assuming a counterparty's capability instead of eliciting it. The mount contract was engineered to a requirement never confirmed as satisfiable.
GOV-010_reviewer_environment_assumed_not.reviewer_was_correct: Refusing to treat GitHub access as equivalent to mount proof.
GOV-010_reviewer_environment_assumed_not.third_occurrence: rev 11 asserted remote availability from a local cache read
GOV-010_reviewer_environment_assumed_not.doctrine: No cross-environment control may become promotion-blocking until the counterparty environment has independently confirmed the required capability exists. Applies to: shared mounts, identical paths, tool availability, network access, credentials, OS features, writable storage.

GOV-011_repository_is_public.observation: github.com/hochster71/hoch_agent_swarm is PUBLIC — unauthenticated clone succeeds. This current mechanism satisfies the required property of independent candidate-byte retrieval.
GOV-011_repository_is_public.consideration: Public visibility is an implementation choice, not a governance invariant. A private repository could satisfy independent retrieval using read-only deploy keys/tokens, checksummed source bundles, or temporary mirrors. As implemented, public visibility is load-bearing for unauthenticated review. A narrow file check proves no tracked .pem files, but repository-wide secret leakage remains NOT ESTABLISHED (requires a full-history secret scan). Founder must verify the public posture is intended and does not conflate public access with independent verification requirements.

GOV-012_technical_cold_review_may_be_unr.observation: Three attempts, three DIFFERENT missing capabilities: (1) worktree outside mount, (2) no filesystem mount, (3) no DNS/network to github.com. Each protocol was designed against an assumption about the reviewer that had not been elicited.
GOV-012_technical_cold_review_may_be_unr.hypothesis: Technical cold review — executing 27 test suites and inspecting a tree — requires a filesystem AND a runtime AND the candidate bytes. If the reviewer runtime provides none of these, no protocol redesign will succeed. The constraint may be the REVIEWER, not the protocol.
GOV-012_technical_cold_review_may_be_unr.required_before_attempt_4: CAPABILITY ELICITATION. Per GOV-010 doctrine, do not design a fourth protocol on assumption. Ask the reviewer what it can actually do, then design to the confirmed capability set — or conclude that technical cold review requires a different reviewer.

GOV-013_ac1_measure_insufficient.weakness: File presence plus a status string does NOT prove the founder made the ratification decision. An ordinary agent write path can satisfy this measure in full without any founder action. The measure verifies an ARTIFACT, not an AUTHORITY.
GOV-013_ac1_measure_insufficient.relation_to_gov007: Same defect family: an assertion that cannot distinguish who produced it. AC1 is to ratification what unsigned commits are to authorship.
required_future_evidence: any mechanism NOT generable by an ordinary agent write path
GOV-013_ac1_measure_insufficient.historical_state: N0_CONSTITUTION DONE is NOT invalidated. Founder ruling: record the weakness, require stronger evidence for future revalidation or promotion.

GOV-014_ac2_adoption_traceability_concur.title: AC2 presence-based measure permits unadopted governance; policy-to-implementation traceability is one-way; concurrency protection incomplete

finding_1_policy_adoption_gap.evidence: EDR-0005 Status: PROPOSED. Repo-wide EDR status census: 8 PROPOSED, 1 ACCEPTED. No document defines who may transition an EDR from PROPOSED to an authoritative state; HELM_CONSTITUTION_v1.0.md defines founder ratification for ITSELF, not for EDRs.
finding_1_policy_adoption_gap.conclusion: Document presence alone does not prove governance adoption.

finding_2_weak_traceability.evidence: scripts/fix_git_governance_commit.sh implements the intended recovery behavior and cites EDR-0005 in its own output. EDR-0005 does NOT name that script as its implementation artifact.
finding_2_weak_traceability.conclusion: Traceability from policy to implementation is one-way only.

demonstrated: stale-lock recovery (rm only after verified unowned)
finding_3_concurrency_protection_incompl.conclusion: Fencing is the actual remaining technical gap.

explicitly_NOT_a_finding.claim: the lsof-guarded recovery script cannot be identified / is a dangling reference
explicitly_NOT_a_finding.disposition: DISPROVEN — not recorded as a finding
explicitly_NOT_a_finding.why: scripts/fix_git_governance_commit.sh lines 14-26 implement exactly the described behavior. The builder's earlier 'cannot be unambiguously identified' was the FOURTH incomplete-search error of this session: filename search for 'recover' plus an isolated lsof grep, without cross-referencing lsof against .git/*.lock. Recorded here so the audit trail shows the claim was raised, tested, and withdrawn BEFORE entry — not corrected afterward.

required_future_measure: EDR-0005 status formally adopted or ratified
authoritative recovery-script path explicitly identified IN EDR-0005 (bidirectional binding)
script proves: lock ownership detection, lsof/equivalent process verification, fail-closed refusal under ambiguity, stale-lock recovery, fencing or equivalent
positive AND negative execution evidence exists
GOV-014_ac2_adoption_traceability_concur.historical_state: N1_WRITE_PATH DONE is NOT invalidated. Requires revalidation under the strengthened measure.

GOV-015_si_evidence_provenance_insuffici.title: System Integration evidence provenance insufficient
GOV-015_si_evidence_provenance_insuffici.separate_from_gov014: concerns EVIDENCE PROVENANCE, not governance adoption. Different remediation and different ownership.
GOV-015_si_evidence_provenance_insuffici.risk: A mutable summary JSON cannot independently establish execution provenance.
GOV-015_si_evidence_provenance_insuffici.corroboration_attempted: searched coordination/evidence and coordination/council for the 284 count — no matching CI record or evidence-ledger entry found

GOV-016_edr_lifecycle_not_codified.title: EDR governance lifecycle not codified
GOV-016_edr_lifecycle_not_codified.scope_note: Concerns the GOVERNANCE LIFECYCLE ITSELF. Independent of GOV-013 (AC1 measure), GOV-014 (AC2 adoption/traceability), GOV-015 (SI evidence provenance).

corpus_census.census_correction: builder initially reported 8 PROPOSED / 1 ACCEPTED = 9 total. Actual total is 11. Two records carry NO status line at all and were silently omitted from the first count — they are not in a non-terminal state, they are outside any lifecycle. EDR-0000 is the foundational four-engines record.

confirmed_absent: handling of legacy records with no status
GOV-016_edr_lifecycle_not_codified.constitution_scope: HELM_CONSTITUTION_v1.0.md defines ratification for ITSELF ("Ratified by Michael Hoch, 2026-07-17"), not for the EDR corpus.

demonstrated_but_not_codified.verbatim_status: ACCEPTED (policy) — Founder-ratified 2026-07-18. Adoption record for HELM Engineering Doctrine v1.0.
demonstrated_but_not_codified.significance: A working transition model EXISTS as an instance. The finding is NOT that no model exists — it is that the model was exercised once and never elevated into an authoritative rule. EDR-0006 is therefore a usable template drawn from the org's own practice, not an invention.
demonstrated_but_not_codified.correction_to_draft: The originally drafted GOV-016 said no artifact defines who may approve/ratify. More precisely: none defines it GENERALLY, while EDR-0006 demonstrates it by example.

GOV-016_edr_lifecycle_not_codified.risk: The governance corpus can evolve into a mixture of proposed records, accepted records, and records with no lifecycle state, with no authoritative mechanism to determine governance status or transition authority.
GOV-016_edr_lifecycle_not_codified.recommended_remediation: Create ONE authoritative EDR lifecycle specification defining: lifecycle states, legal transitions, transition authorities, required evidence, supersession rules, retirement rules, and handling of legacy stateless records. EDR-0006 supplies the template.

GOV-017_readiness_derivation_does_not_pr.title: Factory readiness derivation does not prove executable capability

declared_components_with_zero_implementa.Evidence Auditor: 2 refs, NOT verified as the declared role

confirmed_evidence.historical: scripts/run_helm_multifactory_activation.py seeds M-HRF-01 / T-HRF-01 and carries the in-source comment: 'for factory work that was never dispatched ... anything else is NOT_EXECUTED -- never PASS'

mechanism_verified.note: The finding as drafted said the on_disk condition is satisfied without an executable pipeline. Reading the producer shows the test is thinner still: a single isdir() call. Directory presence -> DEGRADED, absence -> NOT_READY.

GOV-017_readiness_derivation_does_not_pr.risk: A factory can satisfy the source-presence condition while lacking every runtime component required to accept, execute, validate, and publish work. Consumers may read DEGRADED as partially functioning when the declared pipeline does not exist.

required_remediation.principle: capability must be derived SEPARATELY from commercial readiness
required_remediation.ready_rule: READY requires commercial_readiness == READY AND runtime_capability == OPERATIONAL_PROVEN
required_remediation.explicitly: a factory may be operational while commercially degraded; the distinction must be explicit

GOV-017_readiness_derivation_does_not_pr.defect_family: Fourth instance of presence-standing-in-for-capability: AC1 (artifact not authority), AC2 (document not adoption), SI summary (outcome not provenance), readiness (directory not runtime).

attribution_record.inferred: Three declared execution components have no implementation.
attribution_record.not_inferred: HRF can never become operational without redesign.
attribution_record.effect: bounds the finding. The evidence supports MISSING IMPLEMENTATION; it does not support IRRECOVERABLE DESIGN. Without the third line a reader could justify a rewrite the evidence never called for.
reasoning_chain: Inferred:     Runtime capability is not demonstrated.
Not inferred: Architectural redesign is required.
attribution_record.proportionality: because the inference boundary is EXPLICIT, remediation stays proportional to the evidence. The task becomes implementation rather than redesign — determined by the boundary, not chosen.

evidence_update_2026_07_21.resolution: the implementation gap is CLOSED for the exercised path. The finding was not argued away — the missing runtime was built and produced execution evidence.
NOT_resolved: correctness for mission types other than the one exercised
evidence_update_2026_07_21.registry_untouched: HRF readiness remains DEGRADED and readiness_basis remains on_disk=True. Deriving registry state from this run was NOT authorized and was NOT done.
evidence_update_2026_07_21.status: IMPLEMENTATION_GAP_CLOSED — capability demonstrated once, reliability UNKNOWN

finding_renumbering_note: Rev 9 used GOV-005/006/007 for a different mapping. Founder scheme (rev 10) is authoritative; each finding carries a 'was' field. This collision is the same class as GOV-002 and is why every finding now records its prior identifier.

verification_path_analysis.conflation: BILATERAL MOUNT SHAREDNESS and INDEPENDENT CANDIDATE-BYTE VERIFICATION have been treated as one requirement. They are not.

mount_sharedness.proves: two processes resolve the same filesystem path to the same inode
mount_sharedness.achievable: UNKNOWN — depends on infrastructure outside builder control
mount_sharedness.disposition: Founder decision 2026-07-20: NOT marked failed. The infrastructure does not expose the capability. Retained as useful for collaborative debugging, local reproduction, and shared test environments — not for cold review.

candidate_byte_verification.proves: reviewer obtained and validated the exact reviewed bytes without builder mediation

evidence.snapshots_in_tree: both pinned snapshots are committed blobs, byte-identical to disk

remote_proof.method: git ls-remote (network query), then unauthenticated repeat
remote_proof.unauthenticated: SAME result with GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 GIT_ASKPASS=/bin/true — reviewer needs no credentials
remote_proof.prior_error: Rev 11 reported remote availability from `git branch -r --contains`, which reads the LOCAL remote-tracking ref, not the remote. That was GOV-010 recurring inside the very finding that characterized it. Corrected here.

verification_path_analysis.builder_recommendation: A git clone is arguably STRONGER for verification diversity than the shared worktree: the worktree has both parties reading a filesystem the BUILDER can write to, whereas a clone has the reviewer reading an independent service under content-addressed hashes the builder cannot forge. The shared mount was the weaker path for the purpose it was chosen to serve.
verification_path_analysis.founder_decision_required: Whether bilateral MOUNT proof remains a promotion prerequisite, or whether independent CANDIDATE-BYTE verification via the public remote satisfies the diversity requirement. Builder will not drop a founder-set gate unilaterally.

cold_review_attempts.note: reviewer correctly declined the GitHub-as-mount-proof substitution

cold_review_attempts.cause: reviewer runtime could not resolve github.com; no runnable checkout
cold_review_attempts.achieved: independent blob + content retrieval via GitHub connector (different transport)
cold_review_attempts.not_achieved: clean clone, test execution, code inspection

content_boundary.method: reviewer retrieved via GitHub connector API; builder verified via local git objects. Two independent transports, same result.
content_boundary.significance: The nested-path match matters: the reviewer read actual file content, not a summary or a header. Combined with matching blob IDs across two transports, candidate-byte identity is independently corroborated.
content_boundary.explicitly_not: This is NOT a technical review. No tests executed, no code inspected. Content identity != code correctness.

supersedes.why_superseded: BUILD_TO_GOAL.md is less complete than the authoritative AC contract. AC9 in helm_goal.json requires BOTH agent-built adapters AND founder-supplied credentials, and AC9 is listed under externally_gated.

N6_DISPATCH.ac9_measure_verbatim: founder credentials supplied; adapters live; fail-closed until then

workflow.agent_may: draft or maintain the ratification artifact
workflow.invalid_completion: file creation alone does NOT complete this node

proven: founder direction initiated the work (EDR-0005 Context)
a test result exists: 284 passed / 0 failed
not_proven: whether the result is independently reproduced
N2_SI_CORE.prohibited_inference: do NOT infer AGENT merely because it is a test suite

proven: /command implementation contains no explicit founder gate
N7_COMMAND.prohibited_inference: absence of founder language does not prove agent ownership

architectural_conclusion.statement: The organization demonstrates the governance behaviour in individual artifacts but has not yet elevated that behaviour into an authoritative governance rule.

instances.pattern: ratification PRACTISED more strongly than MEASURED
governance INTENT stronger than LIFECYCLE DEFINITION
transition DEMONSTRATED more strongly than DOCUMENTED
SYNCHRONISATION behaviour weaker than the intended AUTHORITY model

architectural_conclusion.relation_to_lifecycle_model: This is the Designed -> Enabled -> Sustained gap observed at the level of governance PRACTICE rather than technical controls.

remediation_dependency_order.current_defect: helm_pert.json has NO updated_at and reports N3_VERIFY=DONE; build_to_goal_status.json reports HOLD with updated_at. Both written by helm_goal_runner.py. Freshness cannot be compared across them.

HRF-RUNTIME-001.title: Implement governed HRF Clarity Brief execution path
do_not: alter the commercial rung to fix this

sequence.1: intake entry point — accepts {query, sources[]}, validates factory identity, policy, budget, artifact destination; emits contemporaneous run_id

sequence.2: Researcher — retrieval via eligible adapters; records routing, citations, costs, failures; MUST NOT silently fall back to manual output

sequence.3: Evidence Auditor — FIRST verify whether the 2 existing refs implement the declared role; reuse only if interface, authority and outputs match the HRF contract; otherwise implement HRF-specific

sequence.4: Synthesis Writer — consumes only evidenced research + audit results; writes the Clarity Brief to the declared artifact path

sequence.5: fact_check_verifier — FAIL-CLOSED gate: citation existence, claim/source alignment, unsupported assertions, required provenance

sequence.6: governed dispatch — route through council/dispatch machinery; enforce SANDBOX_STRICT; enforce $0.50/task before AND during execution

sequence.7: end-to-end proof — re-run the HELM provenance question; require accepted intake, real dispatch, all role outputs, verifier PASS, final artifact, contemporaneous evidence

HRF-RUNTIME-001.council_consequence: HRF NOT AUTHORIZED as a council participant until end-to-end proof passes

ARCH-002_observation_inference_model.title: First-class Observation -> Inference model for all derived status
ARCH-002_observation_inference_model.type: ARCHITECTURAL ENHANCEMENT — not a governance finding
ARCH-002_observation_inference_model.principle: Every derived status must explicitly record BOTH the observed proxy AND the property it claims to represent. Inference becomes visible in the data instead of happening silently in the reader.
ARCH-002_observation_inference_model.benefit: the derivation can later be refined or DISPUTED without changing the underlying observation — proxy and inference are separately auditable
ARCH-002_observation_inference_model.origin: emerged from GOV-013/014/015/017, all four instances of proxy-standing-for-property

core_rule.observation: IMMUTABLE EVIDENCE. Once recorded, never edited.
core_rule.inference: A HYPOTHESIS. May evolve, be refined, or be disputed.
core_rule.consequence: the evidence does not move when the conclusion does

demonstrated_2026_07_20.pattern: on_disk=True remained true; the INTERPRETATION of on_disk=True changed
demonstrated_2026_07_20.invariant: in every case the observation survived while the inference improved

ARCH-001_challenge_before_authoritative.principle: No governance-affecting conclusion becomes authoritative until it has survived an independent challenge phase.

roles.Recorder: writes only conclusions that survive challenge

evidence_this_works: GOV-014 narrowed after the recovery script was found (claim withdrawn pre-entry)
GOV-016 changed from 'no transition model exists' to 'demonstrated but not codified'
GOV-017 strengthened by reading liveness_producer.py instead of the registry description
ARCH-001_challenge_before_authoritative.CAVEAT_role_separation_is_not_independen: Founder framing: 'independence of reasoning, not the number of participants.' BUILDER CAUTION: role separation WITHIN ONE MODEL does not produce independence of reasoning. Same priors, same blind spots, same failure modes -> four confident agreements on one wrong thing. All four of the builder's incomplete-search errors on 2026-07-20 were caught by a DIFFERENT reasoner asking, not by self-review. Prompted skepticism rehearses independence; it does not supply it. REQUIREMENT: where the decision matters, Challenger must be a different MODEL, not a different prompt.
ARCH-001_challenge_before_authoritative.mechanism: decorrelation of error, not quantity of review
ARCH-001_challenge_before_authoritative.revision: rev 20 — founder refinement 2026-07-20: split into two ASSURANCE LEVELS
ARCH-001_challenge_before_authoritative.founder_correction: 'The mistake I made was conflating procedural independence with epistemic independence. Those are different properties.' A Builder/Investigator/Challenger/Recorder architecture on ONE model improves discipline, traceability and separation of concerns — but it must never claim to provide independent verification.

composition: Challenger implemented by a genuinely DIFFERENT reasoning system
preferably different training, search behaviour, or inference characteristics

level_of_this_session.note: the four corrections cited as evidence_this_works were caught at LEVEL 2 — a different reasoner (founder) asked, not self-review. Recording them as Level 1 successes would misattribute the mechanism that actually produced them.

ARCH-001_challenge_before_authoritative.why_the_split_matters: prevents the architecture from overstating what it has achieved. A four-role model built on one model, described as independent verification, would reproduce the failure it exists to prevent — while LOOKING rigorous, which is worse than having no review.

attribution_discipline_confirmed.observed: independent reasoning produced four material corrections
attribution_discipline_confirmed.NOT_inferred: role separation alone would have achieved the same outcome
attribution_discipline_confirmed.founder_2026_07_20: 'avoids a subtle but common mistake: attributing success to the wrong control.' Citing the four corrections as Level 1 evidence would have claimed a capability that was never demonstrated.

ARCH-003_prefer_recomputation_over_corre.recorded_because: authored in chat; recorded at the point of authorship per the same provenance rule applied to the North Star. Recording != adoption. Founder framing: 'one principle I'd consider adding LATER... as a consequence of ARCH-002.'
rule: recompute the inference from the preserved observations
record that the DERIVATION LOGIC changed
ARCH-003_prefer_recomputation_over_corre.pattern: Observation (immutable) -> Derivation v1 -> Conclusion A; same Observation -> Derivation v2 -> Conclusion B
ARCH-003_prefer_recomputation_over_corre.gives: provenance for WHY a conclusion changed, not only THAT it changed
architectural_consequences_of_ARCH_002: evidence stores should be append-only or immutable
derived status can be recomputed whenever derivation logic changes
historical conclusions remain reproducible because they reference fixed observations
improving reasoning does not require rewriting history
ARCH-003_prefer_recomputation_over_corre.note: these are ARCHITECTURAL PROPERTIES, not governance preferences — they constrain storage design
ARCH-003_prefer_recomputation_over_corre.adoption_gate: founder acceptance. Do not treat as binding until status changes.

ARCH-004_evidence_positive_computation.framing_note: founder framing was AFFIRMATIVE ('I would turn this into a general engineering rule') — stronger than ARCH-003's 'consider later'. Recorded as PROPOSED for consistency of process, not because assent is in doubt. Recording != authorizing.
ARCH-004_evidence_positive_computation.rule: State computations should be evidence-positive, never absence-driven.
requirements: never compute from what was NOT FOUND
represent insufficient evidence explicitly as UNKNOWN

Q1_what_is_missing.defect: no natural terminal state of UNKNOWN. Incomplete evidence PRESSURES the reasoner to infer a deficiency — the question manufactures its own answer.

Q2_what_has_been_demonstrated.property: MONOTONIC — new evidence only adds to the demonstrated set or converts an UNKNOWN to KNOWN. It never requires conjecture.

ARCH-004_evidence_positive_computation.compatibility: monotonicity is what makes Q2 compatible with ARCH-002 — observations accumulate, they do not retract
ARCH-004_evidence_positive_computation.existing_implementation: agent_controllable() -> UNKNOWN is this rule in executable form. Not merely conservative: it is the architectural philosophy compiled.

observed_failures_from_violating_it_2026.failure_mode: search stopped before authoritative evidence was exhausted

basis_of_the_rule.but: 'this methodology prevents a repeatedly observed failure mode'
basis_of_the_rule.founder_2026_07_20: the rule is no longer justified only by theory; it is justified by observed failure

investigation_disposition.rationale: backlog defined, architectural principles documented. Further theoretical extension has diminishing return; next meaningful progress comes from EXECUTION.
investigation_disposition.next: HRF-RUNTIME-001 — implement the governed HRF execution path
investigation_disposition.evidence_rule: new runtime evidence updates the EVIDENTIARY sections. It does not extend the theoretical framework.

methodological_shift.difference: the first encourages filling PERCEIVED gaps; the second encourages building only what the evidence shows is missing
consequences: architecture anchored to observable behaviour, not assumptions
methodological_shift.founder_assessment: the most durable outcome of the investigation
methodological_shift.builder_note: this is also what produced UNKNOWN as a first-class value. 'What is missing' cannot return UNKNOWN — it must name a gap, so it invents one. 'What has been demonstrated' returns UNKNOWN honestly. agent_controllable() is that shift compiled.

shape: Inferred:     what is concluded from it
Not inferred: what the evidence deliberately does NOT support
attribution_record_pattern.purpose: the third clause LIMITS the claim. It prevents success from 'bleeding upward' into controls that were present but not demonstrated to be causal.
attribution_record_pattern.first_instance: ARCH-001 — four corrections attributed to Level 2, explicitly NOT to Level 1
attribution_record_pattern.relation_to_ARCH_002: the 'Not inferred' line is where a disputable inference declares its own boundary

state_separation.principle: provenance must distinguish three states that are easily conflated
state_separation.risk: every artifact produced 2026-07-20 — register rev 1..22, HELM_NORTH_STAR.md, governance_states.py, gov007_validate.sh, commit_lease.py, the redactor fix, the datetime.UTC corrections, the CI matrix — exists ONLY in the working tree. Loss of the working tree loses all of it. Committing is a FOUNDER GATE.
state_separation.note: a document may be revised and still have no canonical existence. These are not the same fact.

epistemic_discipline.status: SESSION SYNTHESIS — the durable outcome, above any individual finding
principles: 4. Treat absence as UNKNOWN, not as evidence of nonexistence.
6. Distinguish working state from canonical state.
epistemic_discipline.property: mutually reinforcing — each makes the next enforceable
epistemic_discipline.provenance: every one was EXTRACTED from an observed failure during PROC-001, not designed in advance. That is why they are kept and why the framework should now stop growing: it has no further evidence to feed on.
epistemic_discipline.closing_rule: conclusions now advance only through execution evidence

framework_change_criterion.NOT_a_seventh_principle: deliberately OUTSIDE epistemic_discipline. The six principles describe HOW HELM REASONS FROM EVIDENCE. This describes WHEN THOSE PRINCIPLES MAY THEMSELVES BE REVISED OR EXTENDED. Filing it as a seventh principle would collapse the two layers and make the framework its own amendment authority.
framework_change_criterion.rule: The epistemic framework shall not be extended unless new execution evidence reveals a new recurring mechanism that is not adequately explained by the existing principles.
admission_requirements: the new principle must be TRACEABLE to that evidence in the same manner as the existing framework
rationale: the existing principles were not invented in advance
each was extracted from repeated or structurally significant observed failures
therefore the admission standard for future principles must be IDENTICAL to the standard that produced the current set
framework_change_criterion.preserves: internal consistency of the framework over time
framework_change_criterion.self_application: this criterion was itself authored during the investigation that produced the framework. It is not exempt: revising it requires the same evidentiary standard.

traversal_history.note: HRF had no runtime consumer; blocker classified, remediation routed to HASF
all 4 declared components executed; offline validator passed

traversal_history_rule: APPEND-ONLY. A negative traversal is never removed once a positive one is recorded.
